samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock. Active-low reset. Input enable. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Output enable. Product. 8 Register to store the multiplicand. Register to store the multiplier. Register to store the accumulated sum. Register to store the accumulated sum. Register to store the accumulated sum.

Reset the internal states.

Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock or the falling edge of the reset signal.

When the reset signal is active (low), the multiplicand and multiplier registers are cleared to zero.

if (!rst_n) begin mul_a_reg <= 8'b0; mul_b_reg <= 8'b0; end

When the multiplication enable input signal is active, the multiplicand and multiplier registers are updated with the input values.

else if (mul_en_in) begin mul_a_reg <= mul_a; mul_b_reg <= mul_b; end

Combinational logic block to generate the partial products based on the multiplier bits.

For each bit of the multiplier, a bitwise AND operation is performed with the multiplicand to generate the partial products.

The partial products are stored in the temp array.

wire [7:0] temp [7:0]; assign temp[0] = mul_b_reg[0] ? mul_a_reg : 8'b0; assign temp[1] = mul_b_reg[1] ? mul_a_reg : 8'b0; assign temp[2] = mul_b_reg[2] ? mul_a_reg : 8'b0; assign temp[3] = mul_b_reg[3] ? mul_a_reg : 8'b0; assign temp[4] = mul_b_reg[4] ? mul_a_reg : 8'b0; assign temp[5] = mul_b_reg[5] ? mul_a_reg : 8'b0; assign temp[6] = mul_b_reg[6] ? mul_a_reg : 8'b0; assign temp[7] = mul_b_reg[7] ? mul_a_reg : 8'b0;

Combinational logic block to accumulate the partial products.

For each partial product, it is added to the corresponding bit of the sum register.

The sum register accumulates the partial products.

assign sum[0] = temp[0][0]; assign sum[1] = temp[1][1] + temp[0][1]; assign sum[2] = temp[2][2] + temp[1][2] + temp[0][2]; assign sum[3] = temp[3][3] + temp[2][3] + temp[1][3] + temp[0][3]; assign sum[4] = temp[4][4] + temp[3][4] + temp[2][4] + temp[1][4] + temp[0][4]; assign sum[5] = temp[5][5] + temp[4][5] + temp[3][5] + temp[2][5] + temp[1][5] + temp[0][5]; assign sum[6] = temp[6][6] + temp[5][6] + temp[4][6] + temp[3][6] + temp[2][6] + temp[1][6] + temp[0][6]; assign sum[7] = temp[7][7] + temp[6][7] + temp[5][7] + temp[4][7] + temp[3][7] + temp[2][7] + temp[1][7] + temp[0][7]; assign sum[8] = temp[7][7] + temp[6][7] + temp[5][7] + temp[4][7] + temp[3][7] + temp[2][7] + temp[1][7] + temp[0][7]; assign sum[9] = temp[7][7] + temp[6][7] + temp[5][7] + temp[4][7] + temp[3][7] + temp[2][7] + temp[1][7] + temp[0][7]; assign sum[10] = temp[7][7] + temp[6][7] + temp[5][7] + temp[4][7] + temp[3][7] + temp[2][7] + temp[1][7] + temp[0][7]; assign sum[11] = temp[7][7] + temp[6][7] + temp[5][7] + temp[4][7] + temp[3][7] + temp[2][7] + temp[1][7] + temp[0][7]; assign sum[12] = temp[7][7] + temp[6][7] + temp[5][7] + temp[4][7] + temp[3][7] + temp[2][7] + temp[1][7] + temp[0][7]; assign sum[13] = temp[7][7] + temp[6][7] + temp[5][7] + temp[4][7] + temp[3][7] + temp[2][7] + temp[1][7] + temp[0][7]; assign sum[14] = temp[7][7] + temp[6][7] + temp[5][7] + temp[4][7] + temp[3][7] + temp[2][7] + temp[1][7] + temp[0][7]; assign sum[15] = temp[7][7] + temp[6][7] + temp[5][7] + temp[4][7] + temp[3][7] + temp[2][7] + temp[1][7] + temp[0][7];

Sequential logic block triggered on the rising edge of the clock.

When the multiplication enable input signal is active, the sum register is updated with the temp array.

always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_in) begin for (int i = 0; i < 2*N; i = i + 1) begin sum[i] <= temp[i]; end end end

Combinational logic block to accumulate the final product.

The accumulated sum is assigned to the output register.

always @(*) begin mul_out_reg = sum; end

Output enable logic.

assign mul_en_out = mul_en_out_reg[7];

Output assignment logic.

assign mul_out = mul_en_out ? mul_out_reg : 16'b0;
